title: Scenario Configuration File
text:
Model configure file is Microsoft Windows INI file used to specify scenario settings.


Same settings as the one in model configuration file.

model_folder - The folder where the model files will be created.




The basic settings for scenario.

name - The name of the scenario. A sub-folder will be created with this name in the model folder and all the model files will be in that folder.
model_type - The model type, cell or subarea.
interval - The time interval, daily or hourly
start_date - The start date of simulation in yyyy-mm-dd format. The start date of the hydroclimate database will be used if it’s empty.
end_date - The end date of simulation in yyyy-mm-dd format. The end date of the hydroclimate database will be used if it’s empty.




The climate stations used for the simulation. The required format is ids separated by comma. The ids must match the one in ID column in STATIONS table of hydroclimate database.






Tip



If all hydroclimate stations will be used, leave all the items empty. This is the recommended approach.



P - The stations ids for precipitation.
T - The stations ids for temperature.
RM - The stations ids for relative humidity.
SR - The stations ids for solar radiation.
WS - The stations ids for wind speed.
WD - The stations ids for wind direction. This may be merged with wind speed later.




The method used to generate the weight file.

method - The method for interpolation. Available methods include: average_uniform, grid_interpolation, inverse_distance, linear_triangle and thiessen_polygon.

title: imWEBs: create imWEBs models with Python
text:
.NET Runtime 9.0 is required to run the integrated imWEBsInputTool. The basic runtime for console app should be sufficient.



It’s recommended to install in a separate Python environment.
pip install imwebs



from imWEBs.imwebs import imWEBs
#The first the is the model config file and the second is the scenario config file. imwebs = imWEBs(“imwebs_config_model_stc.ini”,“imwebs_config_scenario_stc.ini”)
imwebs.generate_all()



imWEBs reads model input files and scenario settings from two configuration files. Users should first get familiar with the configuration file content and compile the files before running imWEBs.

Model Configuration File
Scenario Configuration File




imWEBs uses Python logging system to output running info for debugging purpose. It’s recommended to setup the logging before running imWEBs.
import logging.config
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')

title: 41. WASCob
text:
WASCOB (Water and Sediment Control Basin) blocks water from drainage area. The ponded water drains through tile drain system. It always work with the tile drain system.


WASCOB point shapefile is the only input. It specifies the location of the wascob which usually the intercection of the berm and the overland drainage. It’s not neccessary that the point falls exatcly on the drainage path. The system will snap the point to the delineated streams.
WASCOB shapefile must have 14 data fields as shown below. The name must match the ones given below.

id - unique ids
StartYear- the year wascob beomes effective.
StartMon- the month wascob beomes effective.
StartDay- the day wascob beomes effective.
BermElev - berm cres elevation in m
DeadV- the dead volume of the ponding area in 104 m3
DeadA- the dead surface area of the ponding aera in ha
NormalV- the normal volume of the ponding area in 104 m3
NormalA- the normal surface area of the ponding aera in ha
MaxV- the max volume of the ponding area in 104 m3
MaxA- the max surface area of the ponding aera in ha
MaxQ- the discharge capacity in m3/day.
FieldID- the corresponding field
TdrainID - the corresponding tile drain




In the model configuration file, set wascob_outlet_shapefile to the name of the wascob point shapefile.
wascob_shapefile = wascob.shp




wascob is a point shapefile.
id column is included in the shapefile and has unique values.
The tile drain bmp is present and the tile drain id presents in the tile drain shapefile.
All required columns are available in the shapefile.





Use wascob as pour point during watershed delineation
Generate drainage area raster which is essentially the corresponding subbasin.
Generate wascob parameter table. To determine the outlet reach id:

Find the corresponding tile drain field based on tile drain id.
Find the outlet reach for the identified tile drain field and use it for the wacob.

Add wascob drainage area raster to parameter.h5

title: 34. Pasture Crop Land
text:
The pasture crop land BMP is flagged using pasture_crop_land_simulation in pasture_crop_land section. This BMP will be used if it’s set to YES. The default value is NO. One example is given below.
[pasture_crop_land]
pasture_crop_land_simulation = YES



Pature crop lands could be defined by users or created with specified criteria.


The user-defined pasture crop land shapefile could be specified using pasture_crop_shapefile in the pasture_crop_land section. One example is given below.
[pasture_crop_land]
pasture_crop_shapefile = pasture_crop.shp



If the user doesn’t specify the pasture crop land location, the system will use all the lands where the landuse is tame grass. User has option to limit the landuse ids using pasture_crop_land_landuse_ids option. Multiple ids should be separated with comma, e.g. 10, 11, 12. The default value is empty, which means all tame grass landuses will be used.




The grass type applied to pasture crop lands is specified using pasture_crop_land_grass_type section in pasture_crop_land section. One example is given below. The default crop, fertilizer and tillage management data of the specified grass type will be applied to all pasture crop lands.
[pasture_crop_land]
pasture_crop_land_grass_type = 36

title: 29. Manure Feedlot
text:
The location of manure feedlots is defined in non_structure_bmp section with two settings:

manure_feedlot_boundary_shapefile - The feedlot boundary shapefile.
manure_feedlot_outlet_shapefile - The feedlot outlet shapefile.

One example is given below.
[non_structure_bmp]
manure_feedlot_boundary_shapefile = cattle_yard_projected.shp
manure_feedlot_outlet_shapefile = cattle_yard_outlet.shp




feedlot

id - The feedlot id
cb - The catch basin id which must exist in the catch basin shapefile
ani_id - The animal id in the livestock parameter table
adult - Number of the adult animals
nonadult - Number of non-adult animals
stoid - The manure storage ids separated with comma
storatio - The ratio to each manure storage specified in stodi separated with comma. The total ratio should be 1.

Feedlot outlet

id - The outlet id should be the same as the corresponding feedlot

title: 27. Manure Storage
text:
The location of manure storage is defined in [non_structure_bmp] section using manure_storage_boundary_shapefile. One example is given below.
[non_structure_bmp]
manure_storage_boundary_shapefile = storage.shp




id - The manure storage id
feedlot - The contributing feedlot id

text:

title: 27. Manure Storage
text:
id - The manure storage id
feedlot - The contributing feedlot id

title: 19. Tile Drain
text:
Tile drain improve the drainage of fields with poor drainage capacity. It uses underground perforated drain pipes to direct water to an outlet. It may work together with wascobs.


Two shapefiles are required:

Tile drain polygon shapefile to specify the location and attributes of the tile drain fields
Optional tile drain outlet shapefile

The tile drain polygon shapefile must have eight data fields as shown below.

id - unique ids
Depth - drain pipe invert depth in mm
Spacing - drain pipe spacing in mm
Radius - drain pipe radius in mm
Eleation - field elevation in m
StartYear - the start year of operation
StartMon - the start month of operation
StartDay - the start day of operation

The tile drain outlet shapefile will be used as subbasin outlet. It must have an id column with unique ids.



In the model configuration file,

set tile_drain_boundary_shapefile to the name of the tile drain polygon shapefile.
set tile_drain_outlet_shapefile to the name of the tile drain outlet shapefile.

tile_drain_shapefile = tiledrain.shp tile_drain_outlet_shapefile = tiledrain_outlet.shp






The shapefile is a poygon shapefile.
id column is included in the shapefile and has unique values.
all required columns are included in the shapefile and the values are not empty





The shapefile has an id column with unique values.






Use tile drain outlet shapefile as pour point during watershed delineation if it’s defined.
Generate tile drain parameter table. To determine the outlet reach id:

If the tile drain outlet shapefile is defined, find the reach for each outlet and trace from each tile drain field from upstream to downstream. The first outlet is assigned to the tile drain field.
Else find the lowest subbasin included in the tile drain polygon. If the drainage area is smaller than 10 ha, trace downstream until the drainage area is larger than 10 ha.

Add tile drain polygon to parameter.h5

title: 09. Isolated Wetland
text:
Isolated wetland is a reach bmp. Use defines the boundary and optional outlet using two shapefiles in reach_bmp section. One example is given below. Note that the wetland_outlet_shapefile is empty so the interface will delineate the outlet automatically.
[reach_bmp]
wetland_boundary_shapefile = wetland.shp
wetland_outlet_shapefile =



It’s possible to filter the wetlands using a minimum area. This is done by wetland_min_area_ha in delineation section. The following example would skip all wetlands whose area is smaller than 0.1 ha.
[delineation]
wetland_min_area_ha = 0.1

wetland_boundary_shapefile = wetland.shp
wetland_outlet_shapefile =

title: 06 Grassed Waterway
text:
The location of the grassed waterway is defined in [reach_bmp] section using grass_waterway_shapefile. One example is given below. The shapefile must be polyline type.
[reach_bmp]
grass_waterway_shapefile = GWW.shp

title: 06 Grassed Waterway
text:
The location of the grassed waterway is defined in [reach_bmp] section using grass_waterway_shapefile. One example is given below. The shapefile must be polyline type.
[reach_bmp]
grass_waterway_shapefile = GWW.shp

title: 03. Reservoir
text:
The location of reservoirs is defined by a point shapefile. The name of the shapefile is defined in the reach_bmp section of the model configuration file. An example is given below.
[reach_bmp]
reservoir_shapefile = reservoir.shp



The reservoir shapefile should have following attributes.

id - The reservoir id
op - operation date in yyyy-mm-dd format
sa_em - Emergency surface area
v_em - Emergency volume
sa_pr - Principal surface area
v_pr - Principal volume
file - The external file name used for rating curve or outflow time series. File extension is not included.




The routing method is defined in a separate section [reservoir]. This section defines the flow routing method for reservoir and the flow data folder where the external files are located. For RAT_RES, MDO_RES and MMO_RES, external files are required. It’s assumed that these files are all in required csv format available in reservoir_flow_data_folder. The reservoir file column define the file name without extension. The system will read the data and import to bmp database. There are two settings in this section.

reservoir_flow_routing - The flow routing method. Select one of RAT_RES, MDO_RES, MMO_RES, AAR_RES and TRR_RES.
reservoir_flow_data_folder - The folder where the external flow data is saved.

An example is given below.
[reservoir]
reservoir_flow_routing = RAT_RES
reservoir_flow_data_folder = C:\stc\data\reservoir

title: 03. Reservoir
text:

title: 01. Point Source
text:
The location of point source is defined in [reach_bmp] section using point_source_shapefile. One example is given below.
[reach_bmp]
point_source_shapefile = PointSource.shp




Operation - The operation date in YYYY-MM-DD format
TableName - The table name in hydroclimate database that defines time series of the flow, sediment and nutrients. The table must have following columns. The columns are case-insensitive and could be in any sequence.

DATE
FLOW
SEDIMENT
ORGANICN
ORGANICP
NO3
NH3
NO2
MINERALP

riparian_buffer_shapefile = buffer.shp

title: 12. Crop Management
text:
Crop management BMPs specifies the crop rotation, fertilizer application, tillage and gazing on a field. As this data is highly customized, it’s usually prepared manually and imported to bmp database.
AAFC crop inventory provides crop distribution each year based on remote sensing technology. Combined with default crop rotation, fertilizer application, tillage and gazing fro crop type, it’s possible to setup the crop management BMPs automatically. To use this feature, setup the crop_rotation section in the model configuration file in crop_rotation section. The setting items are given below.

method - the only option now is crop_inventory.
AAFC_crop_inventory_folder - the folder the crop inventory GeoTIFF files reside. The file name must be the year, e.g. 2025.tif.
first_year - the starting year of inventory data to read.
last_year - the ending year of inventory data to read.
include_grazing - the flag for grazing generation. By default it’s false.

One example is given below.
[crop_rotation]
method = crop_inventory
AAFC_crop_inventory_folder = C:\Work\Git\imWEBs\imWEBs-Python\notebooks\stc\data\crop_inventory 
first_year = 1991
last_year = 2010
include_grazing = False
Users will need to download the crop inventory data for the years they are interested and clipped to the watershed boundary. It’s also required to have same coordination system as other input files.


The workflow is described below.




The system has default crop lookup table and default management data for crop, fertilizer and tillage. If users wants to use their own data, they could have these files in AAFC_crop_inventory_folder with the following file name. These files must follow the standard formats as given below.

crop-lookup.csv

To simulate the behavior of farmers, plating, harvest, fertilizer and tillage dates could be generate randomly between a starting and ending day defined in the table. In each table, the starting and ending month/day is defined. This is only applied when month and day value is valid month and day values.



The month and day could be negative for fertilizer and tillage. It’s illustrated in the following diagram. In this case, all four month/day column should have the same value.




If the previous year column is 1 in crop_management.csv, the system assumes it’s planted in previous year. For example, if the dominant crop in a field is winter wheat in 2000, the year in crop_management table will be 1999. The imWEBs engine will see it planted in 1999 and harvested in 2000.

title: 12. Crop Management
text: The workflow is described below.

title: 12. Crop Management
text:
The system has default crop lookup table and default management data for crop, fertilizer and tillage. If users wants to use their own data, they could have these files in AAFC_crop_inventory_folder with the following file name. These files must follow the standard formats as given below.

crop-lookup.csv



Original ID
Land use name

title: 12. Crop Management
text: To simulate the behavior of farmers, plating, harvest, fertilizer and tillage dates could be generate randomly between a starting and ending day defined in the table. In each table, the starting and ending month/day is defined. This is only applied when month and day value is valid month and day values.

title: 12. Crop Management
text: The month and day could be negative for fertilizer and tillage. It’s illustrated in the following diagram. In this case, all four month/day column should have the same value.

title: 12. Crop Management
text: If the previous year column is 1 in crop_management.csv, the system assumes it’s planted in previous year. For example, if the dominant crop in a field is winter wheat in 2000, the year in crop_management table will be 1999. The imWEBs engine will see it planted in 1999 and harvested in 2000.

title: 21. Manure Adjustment
text:
Six manure adjustment BMPs are included in imWEBs. They are generated in a similar fashion.

Manure Incorporation within 48 hours
Manure Application Setback
No application on snow
Spring application rather than fall application
Application based on soil nitrogen limit
Application based on soil phosphorus limit







Warning



These BMPs are not supported in the subarea-based engine.





The only input for each BMP is the location specified using a polygon shapefile defined in [manure_adjustment_bmp] section. One example is given below.
[manure_adjustment_bmp]
manure_adjustment_incorporation_within_48h_shapefile = h48.shp
manure_adjustment_application_setback_shapefile = setback.shp
manure_adjustment_no_application_on_snow_shapefile = no_on_snow.shp
manure_adjustment_spring_rather_than_fall_shapefile = spring.shp
manure_adjustment_based_on_n_limit_shapefile = n_limit.shp
manure_adjustment_based_on_p_limit_shapefile = p_limit.shp

title: 21. Manure Adjustment
text:
The only input for each BMP is the location specified using a polygon shapefile defined in [manure_adjustment_bmp] section. One example is given below.
[manure_adjustment_bmp]
manure_adjustment_incorporation_within_48h_shapefile = h48.shp
manure_adjustment_application_setback_shapefile = setback.shp
manure_adjustment_no_application_on_snow_shapefile = no_on_snow.shp
manure_adjustment_spring_rather_than_fall_shapefile = spring.shp
manure_adjustment_based_on_n_limit_shapefile = n_limit.shp
manure_adjustment_based_on_p_limit_shapefile = p_limit.shp

title: 28. Manure Catch Basin
text:
28. Manure Catch Basin
The location of manure catch basins is defined in [reach_bmp] section specified using manure_catch_basin_shapefile. The shapefile should be point and have unique ids. One example is given below.
[reach_bmp]
manure_catch_basin_shapefile = catchbasin.shp

title: 30. Marginal Crop Land
text:
The marginal crop land BMP is flagged using marginal_crop_land_simulation in marginal_crop_land section. This BMP will be used if it’s set to YES. The default value is NO. One example is given below.
[marginal_crop_land]
marginal_crop_land_simulation = YES



Marginal crop lands could be defined by users or created with specified criteria.


The user-defined marginal crop land shapefile could be specified using marginal_crop_shapefile in the marginal_crop_land section. One example is given below.
[marginal_crop_land]
marginal_crop_shapefile = marginal_crop.shp



If the user doesn’t specify the marginal crop land location, the system generates the location using three parameters.

marginal_crop_land_non_agriculture_landuse_ids: the area with specified ids will be used as the base for the marginal crop land. Multiple ids should be separated with comma, e.g. 10, 11, 12. The default value is empty, which means all non-agricultrual landuses will be used.
marginal_crop_land_buffer_size_m: a buffer size from the base area with a default value of 100 m.
marginal_crop_land_slope_threshold_percentage: slope criteria. Areas with smaller slope will be removed. The default value is 7%.





The grass type applied to marginal crop lands is specified using marginal_crop_land_grass_type section in marginal_crop_land section. One example is given below. The default crop, fertilizer and tillage management data of the specified grass type will be applied to all marginal crop lands.
[marginal_crop_land]
marginal_crop_land_grass_type = 36

title: 30. Marginal Crop Land
text:
The marginal crop land BMP is flagged using marginal_crop_land_simulation in marginal_crop_land section. This BMP will be used if it’s set to YES. The default value is NO. One example is given below.
[marginal_crop_land]
marginal_crop_land_simulation = YES

title: 30. Marginal Crop Land
text:
Marginal crop lands could be defined by users or created with specified criteria.


The user-defined marginal crop land shapefile could be specified using marginal_crop_shapefile in the marginal_crop_land section. One example is given below.

If the user doesn’t specify the marginal crop land location, the system generates the location using three parameters.

marginal_crop_land_non_agriculture_landuse_ids: the area with specified ids will be used as the base for the marginal crop land. Multiple ids should be separated with comma, e.g. 10, 11, 12. The default value is empty, which means all non-agricultrual landuses will be used.
marginal_crop_land_buffer_size_m: a buffer size from the base area with a default value of 100 m.
marginal_crop_land_slope_threshold_percentage: slope criteria. Areas with smaller slope will be removed. The default value is 7%.

title: 30. Marginal Crop Land
text:
The grass type applied to marginal crop lands is specified using marginal_crop_land_grass_type section in marginal_crop_land section. One example is given below. The default crop, fertilizer and tillage management data of the specified grass type will be applied to all marginal crop lands.
[marginal_crop_land]
marginal_crop_land_grass_type = 36

title: 39. Offsite Watering
text:
The location offsite watering is defined in non_structure_bmp section using offsite_watering_shapefile. The shapefile must be a point type. This BMP must be used when grazing management is used. One example is given below.
[non_structure_bmp]
offsite_watering_shapefile = offsite_watering.shp

title: 39. Offsite Watering
text:
The location offsite watering is defined in non_structure_bmp section using offsite_watering_shapefile. The shapefile must be a point type. This BMP must be used when grazing management is used. One example is given below.
[non_structure_bmp]
offsite_watering_shapefile = offsite_watering.shp

title: Model Configuration File
text:
Model configure file is a Microsoft Windows INI file used to specify the file source for watershed delineation, bmps and crop rotations. The sections and configures are list below.






Coordination System



All the shapefiles and geotiff files must have the same coordination system.








Raster Format



All raster files must be in GeoTIFF format and have the same resolution and covers same area.








Shapefile ID



All shapefiles must have an id column and have unique values. It’s not case-sensitive so it could id or ID.








Empty Files



If a file is not applicable to the watershed, just leave it empty.








Section and Variable Name



Don’t change the section and variable name.





input_folder - The folder location where all the input files will be read from. All the input files will be in the same folder.




watershed section contains the basic spatial files for the watershed.

dem_raster - The dem raster file.
soil_raster - The soil raster file.
landuse_raster - The landuse raster file.
stream_shapefile - The stream shapefile to burn stream.
boundary_shapefile - The boundary shapefile to limit the watershed boundary.
farm_shapefile - The farm shapefile.
field_shapefile - The filed shapefile.
outlet_shapefile - The output shapefile.







Mandatory Files



dem, soil, landuse, stream, field





Lookup files are csv files that project ids to imWEBs internal ids. The first row is header and the two ids columns are in the first and second column.

soil_lookup - soil lookup csv file to convert the soil id in soil raster to imWEBs soil id.
landuse_lookup - landse lookup csv file to convert the landuse id in landuse raster to imWEBs landuse id.





hydroclimate - The hydroclimate sqlite database.







Hydroclimate Database Format



Only the latest hydroclimate database format is supports. The new format has a STATIONS table and one table each climate data type. If the database is not in this format, use the static method: imWEBs.database.hydroclimate.HydroClimateDatabase.migrate. The input is the file path to the database. The database will be modified directly so backup the old database before running this utility.





This section defines the location and attributes of reach BMPs including point source, flow diversion, reservoir, wetland, manure catch basin, grass waterway, access management and water use.

point_source_shapefile - The point source point shapefile.
flow_diversion_shapefile - The flow diversion point shapefile.
reservoir_shapefile - The reservoir point shapefile.
wetland_boundary_shapefile - The wetland boundary polygon shapefile.
wetland_outlet_shapefile - The optional wetland outlet point shapefile which is not used in most of the time.
manure_catch_basin_shapefile - The manure catch basin point shapefile.
grass_waterway_shapefile - The grass waterway shapefile.
access_management_shapefile - The access management shapefile
water_use_shapefile - The water use shapefile.







Attributes



The attributes of each reach BMP are read from the shapefiles. Depending one the BMP type, the required attributes are different. The system will check these columns and error will happen if the columns are missing.








Known Bug in Copying REAL Attribute Values



The underline library whitebox-workflow has issues coping REAL attribute values. This will be fixed soon in later versions.



reservoir

id - The reservoir id
op - operation date in yyyy-mm-dd format
sa_em - Emergency surface area
v_em - Emergency volume
sa_pr - Principal surface area
v_pr - Principal volume
file - The external file name used for rating curve or outflow time series. File extension is not included.

Manure Catch basin

id - The catch basin id





This section defines the location and attributes of structure bmps.

dugout_boundary_shapefile - The dugout shapefile.
riparian_buffer_shapefile - The riparian buffer shapefile.
filter_strip_shapefile - The filter strip shapefile.
tile_drain_boundary_shapefile - The tile drain shapefile.
tile_drain_outlet_shapefile - The tile drain outlet shapefile.
wascob_shapefile - The WSCOB shapefile.




This section defines the location and attributes of structure bmps.

manure_feedlot_boundary_shapefile - The feedlot boundary shapefile.
manure_feedlot_outlet_shapefile - The feedlot outlet shapefile.
manure_storage_boundary_shapefile - The manure storage boundary shapefile.
offsite_watering_shapefile - The offsite watering shapefile.




feedlot

id - The feedlot id
cb - The catch basin id which must exist in the catch basin shapefile
ani_id - The animal id in the livestock parameter table
adult - Number of the adult animals
nonadult - Number of non-adult animals
stoid - The manure storage ids separated with comma
storatio - The ratio to each manure storage specified in stodi separated with comma. The total ratio should be 1.

Feedlot outlet

id - The outlet id should be the same as the corresponding feedlot

Manure Storage

id - The manure storage id
feedlot - The contributing feedlot id






This section defines the flow routing method for reservoir and the flow data folder where the external files are located. For RAT_RES, MDO_RES and MMO_RES, external files are required. It’s assumed that these files are all in required csv format available in reservoir_flow_data_folder. The reservoir file column define the file name without extension. The system will read the data and import to bmp database.

reservoir_flow_routing - The flow routing method. Select one of RAT_RES, MDO_RES, MMO_RES, AAR_RES and TRR_RES.
reservoir_flow_data_folder - The folder where the external flow data is saved.




The settings for watershed delineation.

stream_threshold_area_ha - The threshold area for stream delineation in ha.
use_all_pour_points_from_stream_threshold - The flag to use all pour points corresponding to stream_threshold_area_ha and structure. If false, a 200ha threshold will be used.
wetland_min_area_ha - The min. wetland area in ha. Wetlands that are smaller than the threshold will be ignored.




The settings for marginal crop land charactorization. Not used.

non_agriculture_landuse_ids - The landuse ids that are not agriculture.
buffer_size_m - The buffer size from the landuse with a default value of 100m
slope_threshold_percentage - The threshold slope percentage of marginal crop land with default value of 7%




The settings for pasture land. Not used.

non_agriculture_landuse_ids - The landuse ids that are charactoraized as pasture




The settings for crop rotation generation. It automatically generate the management tables for crop, fertilizer, tillage and grazing.

method - The method to create the crop rotation. Only crop_inventory is supported now which use the AAFC crop inventory raster to create the crop rotation.
AAFC_crop_inventory_folder - The folder where AAFC crop rotation rasters are saved preferrablely cliped to the waterhsed and have same coordination system as other raster file. The tif files should be named with the year, e.g. 1991.tif.
first_year - The first year to start the crop rotation
last_year - The year to end the crop rotation




The settings for model

model_folder - The folder where the model files will be created.
